pattern: Counting how often each needed letter appears, then taking the most limiting ratio, bounds how many whole copies can be assembled.
hints: Each copy of the goal word consumes a fixed multiset of letters; you can only make as many copies as your scarcest required letter allows.
Tally the frequency of every letter in both strings, then for each letter the goal needs, divide its supply by its demand.
Take the integer floor of supply divided by demand across every distinct letter of the goal, and the smallest of those floors is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: a single letter blocks a second copy
target needs many of one repeated letter
source lacks a required letter entirely
exact single copy with nothing left over
repeated target letter limits despite surplus elsewhere

stress.description: long source over a tiny alphabet with a multi-letter target maximises ratio contention
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    alpha = 'abcde'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))
    tlen = rng.randrange(1, 11)
    target = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(tlen))
    return [s, target]
